raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting a philosophical claim about the nature of time and possession, specifically that one cannot properly relinquish or lose what is not present",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "only the present moment can be properly 'parted with' or possessed",
    "past and future lack the ontological status required for genuine possession or loss",
    "linguistic propriety depends on the temporal status of the referent"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as a definitional or conceptual claim; no logical contradictions within the statement itself",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the audience accepts a present-centered ontology of time or is engaged in ethical/philosophical discourse about relinquishment, memory, or anticipation",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the statement is treated as a literal empirical claim about psychology or physics rather than a normative linguistic or metaphysical assertion, or is applied outside any shared conceptual framework about time"
}
